subject: [PATCH 0/7 v3] mm/memcontrol, page_counter: move stock from mem_cgroup to page_counter

reviews.author: Sashiko
reviews.summary: Raised a concern about racy charges and double-uncharging when racing with concurrent charges. Suggested reordering the callsite to prevent this issue.

reviews.author: Joshua Hahn
reviews.summary: Addressed Sashiko's concerns about racy charges and double-uncharging, explaining that reordering the callsite can prevent this issue.

reviews.author: Sashiko
reviews.summary: Raised additional concerns about synchronous draining with the percpu_charge_mutex lock held, potential ABBA deadlock with mmap_lock, and NOHZ / CPU isolation.

reviews.author: Joshua Hahn
reviews.summary: Addressed Sashiko's concerns about synchronous draining, explaining that it is not a significant issue. Also acknowledged the potential ABBA deadlock and NOHZ / CPU isolation issues.

2026-05-25.patch_summary: This patch series moves the per-cpu stock from mem_cgroup to page_counter, consolidating stock limit checking and page_counter limit checking into page_counter_try_charge. This eliminates the 7-memcg-per-cpu slot limit, random evictions (drain & refill), and slot traversal. The resulting code in memcg is also easier to follow, as the caching becomes transparent from memcg's perspective and managed entirely within page_counter.
